title: Saving seeds from my tomatoes
summary: Explain the small habit of saving tomato seeds each summer to grow new plants next year, free of charge.
topic: Saving seeds from food to grow new plants
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences explaining how you save seeds (or do another small thrifty habit) and grow new food each year. Describe the steps you follow, how often you do it, and why you like it. Use present simple to explain the process and 'and'/'because' to link your steps and reasons.
hintStarters: Every summer, when my tomatoes are ripe, I…
I dry the seeds on a paper towel because…
Next spring, I plant them and…
sampleText: Every summer, when my tomatoes are ripe, I save the seeds. I cut one good tomato in half and take out the small seeds with a spoon. I wash them in a little water and clean off the soft red part. Then I dry the seeds on a paper towel because wet seeds go bad quickly. After a few days, the seeds are dry and hard. I put them in a small paper envelope and write the date on it. I always label the envelope because I forget which seeds are which. I keep the envelope in a cool, dark drawer all winter. Next spring, I plant the seeds in soil near my window, and I water them every morning. In a few weeks, small green plants grow, and they are strong and healthy. I love this habit because it costs me nothing, and the new tomatoes taste the same as the old ones. One ripe tomato in summer gives me many plants for the next year. A small thing in my hand today becomes food on my table tomorrow.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: take out the small seeds with a spoon
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: lấy những hạt nhỏ ra bằng một cái thìa

sampleAnnotations.phrase: dry the seeds on a paper towel
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: phơi khô hạt trên một tờ khăn giấy
một cái phong bì giấy nhỏ
tôi luôn ghi nhãn lên phong bì

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for a process
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi giải thích các bước, dùng hiện tại đơn với 'I': 'I cut', 'I wash', 'I dry', 'I put'. Các từ nối thời gian như 'then', 'after a few days', 'next spring' giúp sắp xếp các bước.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: When you explain steps, use the present simple with 'I': 'I cut', 'I wash', 'I dry', 'I put'. Time words like 'then', 'after a few days', 'next spring' help order the steps.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'because' to explain a step
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'because' để giải thích vì sao bạn làm một bước: 'I dry the seeds because wet seeds go bad quickly.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'because' to explain why you do a step: 'I dry the seeds on a paper towel because wet seeds go bad quickly.'

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi mùa hè, khi cà chua của tôi chín, tôi giữ lại hạt giống. Tôi cắt một quả cà chua ngon làm đôi và lấy những hạt nhỏ ra bằng một cái thìa. Tôi rửa chúng trong một ít nước và làm sạch phần đỏ mềm. Sau đó tôi phơi khô hạt trên một tờ khăn giấy vì hạt ướt nhanh bị hỏng. Sau vài ngày, hạt khô và cứng. Tôi cho chúng vào một cái phong bì giấy nhỏ và viết ngày tháng lên đó. Tôi luôn ghi nhãn lên phong bì vì tôi hay quên hạt nào là hạt nào. Tôi giữ phong bì trong một ngăn kéo mát và tối suốt mùa đông. Mùa xuân tới, tôi trồng hạt xuống đất gần cửa sổ, và tôi tưới chúng mỗi sáng. Trong vài tuần, những cây xanh nhỏ mọc lên, và chúng mạnh khỏe. Tôi yêu thói quen này vì nó không tốn của tôi gì cả, và những quả cà chua mới có vị giống hệt những quả cũ. Một quả cà chua chín vào mùa hè cho tôi nhiều cây cho năm sau. Một điều nhỏ trong tay tôi hôm nay trở thành thức ăn trên bàn của tôi ngày mai.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best opens the process?
options: Every summer, when my tomatoes is ripe, I save the seeds.
Every summer, when my tomatoes are ripe, I saves the seeds.
Every summer, when my tomatoes are ripe, the seeds I save them.
Every summer, when my tomatoes are ripe, I save the seeds.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'tomatoes are' is plural agreement; with 'I' the verb is 'save' (no -s).

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the first step most naturally?
options: I cut one good tomato in half and take out the small seeds with a spoon.
I cut one good tomato in half and takes out the small seeds with a spoon.
I cuts one good tomato in half and take out the small seeds with a spoon.
I cut one good tomato in half and out take the small seeds a spoon with.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both verbs are base form with 'I' ('cut', 'take'), and the order is natural.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence explains a step with 'because' correctly?
options: I dry the seeds on a paper towel because of wet seeds go bad quickly.
I dry the seeds on a paper towel because wet seeds go bad quickly.
I dry the seeds on a paper towel because go bad quickly wet seeds.
Because I dry the seeds wet seeds go bad quickly on a paper towel.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' is followed by a full clause: 'wet seeds go bad quickly'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because' you need a complete clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple: 'After a few days, the seeds ___ dry and hard.'
options: is
be
are
being

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes labelling, with a reason?
options: I always labels the envelope because I forget which seeds are which.
I label always the envelope because I forget which seeds are which.
I always label the envelope because which seeds are which I forget it.
I always label the envelope because I forget which seeds are which.
mcQuestions.explanation: With 'I' the verb is 'label' (no -s); 'always' goes before it; the reason clause is correct.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb form and the place of 'always'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes planting in spring most naturally?
options: Next spring, I plant the seeds in soil near my window, and I water them every morning.
Next spring, I plants the seeds in soil near my window, and I waters them every morning.
Next spring, I plant the seeds in soil near my window, and I water them every mornings.
Next spring, in soil near my window the seeds I plant, and every morning them I water.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both verbs are base form with 'I', and word order is natural.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the new plants correctly?
options: In a few weeks, small green plants grows, and they is strong and healthy.
In a few weeks, small green plants grow, and they are strong and healthy.
In a few weeks, small green plants grow, and they are strong and healthily.
In a few weeks, small green plants grow, and strong and healthy they are it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'plants grow' (plural, no -s) and 'they are' agree; 'healthy' is the correct adjective.
mcQuestions.hint: Match the plural subjects and use adjectives after 'are'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence explains why you love the habit?
options: I love this habit because of it costs me nothing.
I loves this habit because it cost me nothing.
I love this habit because it costs me nothing.
I love this habit because nothing me it costs.
mcQuestions.explanation: With 'I' use 'love'; 'because it costs me nothing' is a correct reason clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb forms and the clause after 'because'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence shows how a small thing grows over time?
options: One ripe tomato in summer give me many plants for the next year.
One ripe tomato in summer gives me many plant for the next year.
One ripe tomato in summer gives me many plants for the next year forever globally.
One ripe tomato in summer gives me many plants for the next year.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'gives' agrees with the singular 'one ripe tomato', and 'plants' is plural.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb ending and the plural noun.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best closes the explanation?
options: A small thing in my hand today becomes food on my table tomorrow.
A small thing in my hand today become food on my table tomorrow.
A small thing in my hand today becomes food on my table tomorrow already.
A small thing in my hand today is becomes food on my table tomorrow.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'becomes' agrees with the singular subject 'a small thing'.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb agreement with the singular subject.

criticalThinkingQuestion: If everyone saved a few seeds from their food each year, how might that change the way we think about buying and growing things?
